layers.title: Define Operator Workflow
layers.summary: The design and plan documents establish the intended operator workflow before code is introduced. They specify a local, read-only `dscli logs -w <host:port> [-n N]` command that resolves workers using the existing `-worker_address=` signal and tails the corresponding local log file.
layers.diff:
+Add a read-only `dscli logs` subcommand that prints the last N lines of a
+datasystem worker's log file, selected by the worker's address.
+
+1. **Add `cli/logs.py`** -- a `Command(BaseCommand)` subclass:
+   - `name`/`description`, and `add_arguments` for `-w/--worker_address`
+     (required) and `-n/--lines` (default 100).
+   - `run(args)`: resolve the worker by address, locate its log file under the
+     worker log directory, and print the last N lines to stdout.
+
+**Out of scope (YAGNI):**
+- Follow mode (`tail -f` / `-f`).
+- Multi-worker or remote-host aggregation.
+- Filtering / grep (operators can pipe to `grep`).

layers.title: Implement Logs Command
layers.summary: The backend CLI layer adds a `BaseCommand` subclass that accepts a worker address and optional tail length. It checks for a matching running worker with `pgrep`, warns if absent, then shells out to `tail` against the expected worker log path and prints stdout.
layers.diff:
+class Command(BaseCommand):
+    """Print the last N lines of a worker's log file to stdout."""
+
+    name = "logs"
+    description = "print recent log lines for a worker service"
+
+    def add_arguments(self, parser):
+        parser.add_argument(
+            "-w", "--worker_address", metavar="ADDR", required=True,
+            help="address (host:port) of the worker whose log to print",
+        )
+        parser.add_argument(
+            "-n", "--lines", metavar="N", default=_DEFAULT_LINES,
+            help="number of trailing log lines to print",
+        )
+
+    def run(self, args):
+        address = args.worker_address
+        if self.find_worker_pid(address) is None:
+            self.logger.warning(f"No running worker @ {address}; showing last log")
+
+        log_path = os.path.join(_LOG_DIR, f"{address}.log")
+        result = subprocess.run(
+            f"tail -n {args.lines} {log_path}",
+            shell=True, capture_output=True, text=True,
+        )

layers.title: Register Subcommand
layers.summary: After the command implementation exists, the CLI dispatcher exposes it by adding `logs` to the module list. This makes the new command available through the existing `dscli` command-loading path without changing existing command signatures.

layers.title: Cover Primary Path
layers.summary: The test layer mocks worker discovery and the tail subprocess so the command can be exercised without a real worker or log file. It verifies that a successful tail returns `BaseCommand.SUCCESS`, covering the basic running-worker success path.
layers.diff:
+class TestLogsCommand(unittest.TestCase):
+    """Tests for :class:`yr.datasystem.cli.logs.Command`."""
+
+    @mock.patch("yr.datasystem.cli.logs.subprocess.run")
+    @mock.patch.object(logs.Command, "find_worker_pid", return_value=4321)
+    def test_run_prints_recent_lines(self, _mock_pid, mock_run):
+        """A running worker's log tail is printed and the command succeeds."""
+        mock_run.return_value = mock.Mock(returncode=0, stdout="a\nb\nc\n", stderr="")
+
+        cmd = logs.Command()
+        args = mock.Mock(worker_address="127.0.0.1:31501", lines=100)
+        rc = cmd.run(args)
+
+        self.assertEqual(rc, logs.BaseCommand.SUCCESS)

summary: This PR adds a read-only `dscli logs` subcommand that locates a worker by address and prints recent lines from its local log file, with focused unit coverage and design documentation.
